verses.2: “Should a wise man answer with vain knowledge,
verses.3: Should he reason with unprofitable talk,
verses.4: Yes, you do away with fear,
verses.5: For your iniquity teaches your mouth,
verses.6: Your own mouth condemns you, and not I.
verses.7: “Are you the first man who was born?
verses.8: Have you heard the secret counsel of God?
verses.9: What do you know that we don’t know?
verses.10: With us are both the gray-headed and the very aged men,
verses.11: Are the consolations of God too small for you,
verses.12: Why does your heart carry you away?
verses.13: that you turn your spirit against God,
verses.14: What is man, that he should be clean?
verses.15: Behold, he puts no trust in his holy ones.
verses.16: how much less one who is abominable and corrupt,
verses.17: “I will show you, listen to me;
verses.18: (which wise men have told by their fathers,
verses.19: to whom alone the land was given,
verses.20: the wicked man writhes in pain all his days,
verses.21: A sound of terrors is in his ears.
verses.22: He doesn’t believe that he will return out of darkness.
verses.23: He wanders abroad for bread, saying, ‘Where is it?’
verses.24: Distress and anguish make him afraid.
verses.25: Because he has stretched out his hand against God,
verses.26: he runs at him with a stiff neck,
verses.27: because he has covered his face with his fatness,
verses.28: He has lived in desolate cities,
verses.29: He will not be rich, neither will his substance continue,
verses.30: He will not depart out of darkness.
verses.31: Let him not trust in emptiness, deceiving himself,
verses.32: It will be accomplished before his time.
verses.33: He will shake off his unripe grape as the vine,
verses.34: For the company of the godless will be barren,
verses.35: They conceive mischief and produce iniquity.
